transcript:
Hi everyone. I'm Sylvie, I'm the co-founder and CEO of Atomiq Labs and we're building a cross-chain settlement layer that is Bitcoin secured.

Imagine this. You're traveling in this dark alley over here. You don't have cash, you need local cash. You walk into one of these like little bit shady looking money exchange places. You hand over a thousand US dollars to them and they tell you, they take it, they tell you, 'Well, be right back. We just need to step outside for a second. We get your change.' You would tell them like, 'Hell no. Give me my cash back.'

Well, this is pretty much what's happening in the cross-chain world today. We hand over our tokens to opaque validator and multisig based cross-chain solutions and then we pray basically that they don't rug us.

So yes, that's the state of cross-chain today and this is what we want to change with Atomiq because we want to stop this 'trust me bro' era of crypto.

What do we do differently? We basically use Bitcoin as the ultimate source of truth. So if you want to swap SOL into BTC, you lock up your SOL in a smart contract vault on Solana that can only be unlocked by the counterparty with a successful Bitcoin transaction. Why is this so secure? Well, you basically would have to cheat Bitcoin's proof of work in order to cheat Atomiq. And good luck capturing 51% of the Bitcoin hash rate because it's much more expensive than what you could actually steal.

We're live on atomiq.exchange with swaps between Bitcoin, on-chain and Lightning, and Solana, SOL, USDC, WBTC and BONK, Bitcoin secured and with zero slippage.

Here we're actually buying a Bitcoin conference ticket with USDC on Solana and the vendor received um Lightning. Well, okay, seems like the video is not working. Sorry about that.

Yeah, well, very sad. I had a video over here that basically showed um a Lightning payment. It took less than 3 seconds. We paid in USDC and they received Bitcoin Lightning.

What's next for Atomiq? Confidential, Bitcoin-secured swaps between any two chains, for example Ethereum and Solana, for retail, institutions and beyond with zero slippage.

Thanks a lot for having me, Breakpoint and Superteam, and yeah, looking back, looking forward to being back at Breakpoint next year.

# Solana Breakpoint 2025: Key Announcements and Stories from Saturday
Day Three maintained the high expectations with announcements from Solflare, Solana Mobile, and Colosseum, among others.
[Pablo](https://solanafloor.com/author/pablo)
Copy link
* Published:
* Edited:
It was said that this year’s Breakpoint would be Solana’s biggest conference yet, and I’m sure none of the several thousand delegates that made their way to the Etihad Arena this week would disagree with that sentiment.
Saturday’s session featured a diverse set of updates highlighting Solana’s continued expansion across hardware, real-world assets, and institutional finance, as well as side events such as Predictify, hosted by[Kalshi](https://x.com/Kalshi?s=20).
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999766576091025438?s=20)> The last day of Breakpoint 2025 delivered a mix of hackathon results, product demonstrations, and strategic commitments that underscored the network’s growing role as a settlement layer for both digital and traditional assets.
Here’s what you missed if you’re not in Abu Dhabi:
## At Breakpoint
1. Colosseum has announced winners for its Cypherpunk hackathon
* *Unruggable, the Solana-native hardware wallet, is crowned grand champion.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999765402453467212?s=20)> 1. Uranium Digital demoed their product, which enables institutions to buy Uranium onchain for the first time.
* *Settlement is happening fully on Solana*
* *The entire crypto experience is abstracted*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999744679441826044?s=20)> 1. RockawayX launches 3 vaults on Solana.
* *Risk-curated RWA vault on Kamino*
* *Additional vaults on Exponent Finance and MidasRWA*
* *Partners with Figure, Onre, Solstice, and Huma as professional curators for these vaults.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999753859456204968?s=20)> 1. Solana Mobile is expanding beyond its own devices to additional hardware.
* *Working with FXTech, MediaTek, and Trustonic to integrate the Solana Mobile stack at the chipset level for other Android devices.*
* *MediaTek ships roughly 50% of all Android devices globally.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999757725505642967?s=20)> 1. Splyce Finance introduces $SFULC on Solana.
* *$SFULC is a yield-bearing token offering 10% APY powered by Fulcrum Lending*
* *Powered by real cash flows from senior, secured multifamily credit.*
* *$SFULC can be minted with stablecoins or bought directly on Raydium.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999761140931526825?s=20)> 1. VC Firm 6th Man Ventures announces that it is focused on Solan

# Agave 3.0: What Does a Solana’s 40% Performance Boost Means for Builders
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/62624e283b503f0ce727563e/688c94b72182390130cda8b4_photo_2025-06-26_14.16.26.avif)
Written by
[
Ted Bloquet
](https://tatum.io/blog-authors/ted-bloquet)
November 3, 2025
6
min. read
![Solana 40% boost with Agave 3.0](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/62624e283b503f0ce727563e/691eb356e47046d6459313d4_Blog%20(10).jpg)
Table of Contents
[
Ethereum
](#)
## ‍**Solana Levels Up Again**
Solana has taken another big step forward with**Agave 3.0**, the latest release of its core validator client, developed by**Anza**, one of the main engineering teams behind the network.
This version focuses on what Solana does best: speed and efficiency. It delivers faster transaction processing, better compute scaling, and smoother real-time communication between validators and applications.
For anyone building on Solana through[**Tatum’s RPC Gateway**](https://tatum.io/nodes)**,**[**Data API**](https://tatum.io/blockchain-api)**, or**[**Notifications**](https://tatum.io/notifications), this upgrade means your app just got faster, automatically.
## **Runtime and Network Performance Reimagined**
Agave 3.0 comes with a full runtime cache redesign that makes block execution leaner and more predictable.
By removing repetitive operations, transaction batches now complete up to**40% faster**, significantly improving network responsiveness.
The update also raises the limit on how much compute a single account can use in a block. Developers pushing the boundaries of smart contract logic, such as trading platforms or yield protocols, can now execute more complex operations without hitting old compute ceilings.
On the networking side, Agave integrates**eXpress Data Path (XDP)**, a low-level optimization that allows Solana’s data-sharing layer (Turbine) to transmit information much faster between validators.
The end result is lower latency across the network, meaning faster confirmations, quicker WebSocket updates, and steadier throughput even when traffic surges.
Through Tatum’s[Solana RPC endpoints](https://docs.tatum.io/reference/rpc-solana), these improvements are instantly reflected in your API calls. You don’t need to reconfigure anything, your requests simply resolve faster.
## **Deeper Composability for On-Chain Programs**
One of the most developer-friendly updates in Agave 3.0 is the increase in**Cross-Program Invocation (CPI)**depth, doubled from four to eight levels.
This allows on-chain programs to interact with each other more freely, enabling complex DeFi logic, multi-step swaps, and advanced wallet or gaming use cases to run within a single transaction.
For builders using**SDK or direct RPC methods**, that means fewer failed invocations and smoother composability between smart contracts.
## **Simplified Scheduling and Faster Validator Recovery**
Agave 3.0 also makes it easier for Solana’s leaders to pack transactions efficiently into each block.
A new scheduling model allows transactions that touch the same account to run sequentially rather than invalidating each other, paving the way for future asynchronous execution.
The new lightweight “TransactionView” structure further cuts down on how much work validators do when parsing and verifying transactions.
Validators themselves now restart in roughly**half the time**. Snapshot recovery is down to just a few minutes, improving uptime across the network and making Solana’s infrastructure even more resilient.
For Tatum, which runs distributed[Solana nodes](https://tatum.io/cha

sources.title: CreatorFi Launches on Aptos with $2M Strategic Backing to Scale Stablecoin Credit for Creators

# CreatorFi Launches on Aptos with $2M Strategic Backing to Scale Stablecoin Credit for Creators
## News provided by
[**CreatorFi**](https://www.prnewswire.com/news/creatorfi/)
Nov 25, 2025, 09:29 ET

NEW YORK,Nov. 25, 2025/PRNewswire/ --**[CreatorFi](https://creatorfi.finance/)**, the innovative platform enabling creators, artists, gaming studios, and media businesses to access credit against their digital IP, today announced a strategic partnership with[Aptos Foundation](https://aptosnetwork.com/)and[Aptos Labs](http://www.aptoslabs.com/). As part of the collaboration, CreatorFi will launch its platform on Aptos, one of the fastest-growing Layer 1 blockchains, and receive a total of $2 million in strategic funding to accelerate growth.
[Continue Reading
**](#continue-jump)
[![CreatorFi Launches on Aptos with $2M Strategic Backing to Scale Stablecoin Credit for Creators]( "CreatorFi Launches on Aptos with $2M Strategic Backing to Scale Stablecoin Credit for Creators")](#)
CreatorFi Launches on Aptos with $2M Strategic Backing to Scale Stablecoin Credit for Creators
The partnership positions CreatorFi as a first-of-its-kindstablecoin-based lending platform, enabling creators and businesses to borrow and receive payouts entirely in USDC. By leveraging Aptos' cross-chain transfer protocols and fiat on/off ramps, CreatorFi brings seamless, capital-efficient lending to the creator economy.
"Our mission has always been to unlock the value of creator IP and make access to capital simple, efficient, and global," said Jack Cameron, Chief Business Officer and co-founder of CreatorFi. "Partnering with Aptos Foundation and Aptos Labs allows us to scale this mission on a blockchain that shares our vision for on-chain finance,stablecoin-powered transactions, and real economic activity. Together, we're not just building credit products for creators—we're demonstrating how the future of global digital finance can work."
"CreatorFi is the first platform to intelligently underwrite and finance the growth of the creator economy, a massively successful and fast growing market that has been historically underserved by traditional lenders," said Ash Pampati, SVP, Head of Ecosystem at Aptos Foundation and former Global Head of Go-To-Market for YouTube Music. "This is exactly the kind of real-world,stablecoin-powered utility Aptos was built to support, and we're excited to help CreatorFi set a new standard for what onchain lending can achieve."
**Key Highlights of the Partnership:**
* **StablecoinLoan Infrastructure:**CreatorFi embeds USDC directly into the credit workflow. Borrowers receive and repay loans in USDC, with optional fiat off-ramps, fully leveraging Aptos' integration of Circle's USDC and CCTP protocols.
* **Proof of Credit on Aptos:**CreatorFi targets creator IP cashflows as collateral, expanding Aptos'DeFiecosystem beyond traditional lending verticals and diversifying its onchain credit stack.
* **Unparalleled Marketing Reach:**CreatorFi's borrower base spans billions of impressions across games, media, and content platforms, introducing Aptos to new audiences while demonstrating real-world utility.
* **Institutional-Grade Synergy:**The platform complements Aptos Foundation's collaborations with institutional players, offering structured, yield-bearing products for regulated lenders and reinforcing the network as a hub for transparent web3 finance.
With a $25 million warehouse facility scalable to $100 million, CreatorFi's launch on Aptos demonstrates the

tags.impact_reason: Atomiq Labs offers a cross-chain settlement layer secured by Bitcoin, enabling swaps between Solana and Bitcoin; this is a solid update, but needs more adoption to have a higher impact.

This illustrates the core concept of a trustless connection between two different worlds. I included the diegetic text '0%' integrated into the light bridge itself to highlight the key user benefit of 'zero-slippage' swaps mentioned in the standfirst.

Extending from it is a shimmering, solid bridge of crystalline light that connects to a sleek, floating data-structure glowing with Solana's purple and green neon. This bridge is the 'Light Client'. Integrated diegetically within the bridge's glowing lattice structure are the characters '0%', representing zero-slippage. The background is a dark, digital void, creating immense depth and focusing all attention on the secure, illuminated connection.

Extending from its colossal form is a shimmering, solid bridge of crystalline light, acting as the 'Light Client', which connects to a sleek, floating data-structure glowing intensely with Solana's signature purple and green neon. Integrated diegetically within the glowing lattice structure of the crystalline bridge are luminous, ethereal characters forming '0%', representing zero-slippage, glowing with an internal light. The background is an expansive, dark, digital void, creating immense depth and focusing all attention on the secure, illuminated connection. The scene is bathed in dramatic, high-contrast lighting with a single, powerful light source highlighting the shimmering bridge and the glowing data structure, while the basalt monolith casts sharp, well-defined shadows, emphasizing its monumental scale and deep textures. High falloff ensures everything outside the key illuminated areas falls into profound darkness, creating a cinematic mood. The composition emphasizes height, epic scale, and significant negative space at the top, making the subject feel monumental. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photoreal, realistic materials, cinematic contrast.

editorial.headline: Atomiq Labs Deploys Bitcoin Light Client on Solana for Trustless Settlement
editorial.standfirst: The protocol deprecates multisig bridges by allowing Solana smart contracts to cryptographically verify Bitcoin Proof-of-Work directly, enabling zero-slippage cross-chain swaps.
key_takeaways: Security Architecture: Utilizes a Bitcoin light client on Solana to verify transactions, removing reliance on multisig validators and requiring a 51% attack on Bitcoin to compromise funds.
Performance: Live integration supports sub-3-second settlement from USDC on Solana to the Bitcoin Lightning Network.
Roadmap: Upcoming expansion includes confidential, Bitcoin-secured swaps between any two chains (e.g., Ethereum and Solana) targeting institutional privacy needs.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a crowded market of cross-chain interoperability protocols, **Atomiq Labs** has delivered a distinct technical breakthrough at Solana Breakpoint 2025: a functional Bitcoin light client running directly on the Solana network. CEO **Sylvie Durach** framed the current state of bridging—dominated by opaque multisigs and validator sets—as a "dark alley" transaction where users hand over assets and hope for the best. Atomiq’s architecture eliminates this counterparty risk by treating Bitcoin’s Proof-of-Work as the ultimate source of truth.

Unlike traditional bridges that rely on a federation of signers to attest to state changes, Atomiq’s protocol allows a smart contract vault on Solana to independently verify Bitcoin transactions. To unlock assets on the Solana side, a counterparty must provide cryptographic proof of a successful Bitcoin transaction. This mechanism binds the security of the swap to Bitcoin’s hashrate; compromising the protocol would theoretically require a 51% attack on the Bitcoin network itself, a threshold significantly higher than the economic cost of corrupting a standard multisig committee.

The protocol is currently live on **atomiq.exchange**, facilitating swaps between Solana assets (SOL, USDC, WBTC, BONK) and Bitcoin (both on-chain and Lightning). During the presentation, Durach described a live demo where a conference ticket was purchased using USDC on Solana, with the vendor receiving Bitcoin via the Lightning Network in under three seconds. This integration effectively positions Solana as a high-speed execution layer for Bitcoin liquidity, abstracting the friction of traditional atomic swaps while maintaining a trustless security model.

Looking forward, Atomiq is targeting the institutional sector with the development of confidential swaps. This feature aims to enable private, Bitcoin-secured settlement between any two chains, such as Ethereum and Solana. For institutional investors and OTC desks, this promises a mechanism to execute large-scale cross-chain rebalancing without exposing trade intent to the public mempool or relying on centralized intermediaries.
